description.en: By default when using getboolean(), config parsers consider the following values True: '1', 'yes', 'true', 'on' and the following values False: '0', 'no', 'false', 'off'. You can override this by specifying a custom dictionary of strings and their Boolean outcomes. For example: >>> custom = configparser.ConfigParser() >>> custom['section1'] = {'funky': 'nope'} >>> custom['section1'].getboolean('funky') Traceback (most recent call last): ... ValueError: Not a boolean: nope >>> custom.BOOLEAN_S...

description.en: The default implementation used by ConfigParser. It enables values to contain format strings which refer to other values in the same section, or values in the special default section [1]. Additional default values can be provided on initialization. For example: [Paths] home_dir: /Users my_dir: %(home_dir)s/lumberjack my_pictures: %(my_dir)s/Pictures [Escape] # use a %% to escape the % sign (% is the only character that needs to be escaped): gain: 80%% In the example above, ConfigParser with i...
